overview: Flux 2 rewards photographic precision — describe your scene like a photography brief with camera body, lens, and lighting setup. CRITICAL: Flux 2 does NOT support negative prompts (that was Flux 1). Instead, describe what you want in affirmative language: 'sharp focus throughout' not 'no blur'. Word order matters: put your main subject first, then action, then style, then context. Natural language prose outperforms keyword lists. Sweet spot: 30-80 words.

style_specificity.description: Camera body, lens, film stock — these single terms unlock huge style shifts. Subject must come FIRST in the prompt (Flux 2 pays most attention to what comes first).

prompt_structure.description: Word order: Subject → Action → Style → Context. Flux 2 weights earlier words more heavily. Use natural language prose, not comma-separated keywords.

technical_photography.description: Photography technical terms that map to real lens/sensor physics. For text in image, use quotation marks: 'The word OPEN appears on the sign'.

lighting.description: Lighting described precisely. Flux 2 responds to both studio and natural setups.

subject_detail.description: Flux 2 rewards verbosity. Describe clothing texture, material, background details. 30-80 words is the sweet spot.

power_patterns: [Subject description] [doing action], [lighting setup], shot on [camera body], [lens], [film/aesthetic]. [Mood/atmosphere].
Portrait of [detailed subject description], [environment], [lighting direction and quality], shot on [camera], [lens], [color grade].
antipatterns: CRITICAL: Negative prompts do NOT work in Flux 2. Use affirmative language instead — 'sharp focus throughout' not 'no blur'.
Putting style before subject — Flux 2 weights the first words most. Subject always first.
Keyword lists without context — Flux 2 is a natural language model. Write prose, not 'woman, forest, dramatic, amazing'.
Conflicting depth of field — 'f/1.4 bokeh AND everything sharp' confuses the simulation.
Missing the camera reference — one of the fastest single-word style levers in Flux and most prompts skip it entirely.

strong: Environmental portrait of a chef in a commercial kitchen, flour-dusted apron, intent expression, harsh overhead fluorescent mixed with warm window fill light, shot on Canon 5D Mark IV, 35mm f/2, ISO 1600, fine grain. Natural, unglamorous.
An elderly man reading in a library window, afternoon light cutting through dust particles, shot on Hasselblad 500CM, 80mm, Kodak Portra 400, warm golden tones, cinematic.
weak: A nice photo of a chef cooking, no blurriness, no bad lighting.
Beautiful, amazing, photorealistic woman in forest, no background noise.
